english: come in / in here (direction from outside to inside)
notes: Used to indicate movement from outside to inside, often as an interjection meaning 'come in!' when someone knocks. Commonly used as a prefix in separable verbs like 'hereinkommen', 'hereinlassen', 'hereintreten'.

sentences.german: Er kam herein und setzte sich ans Fenster.
sentences.english: He came in and sat down by the window.
